title_guess: Exmoor weekend: Halse Farm, Winsford Hill, Brandon Hills, Queens Camel

captions: Looking up the Punch Bowl, Winsford Hill. We walked around this.
pages.text:
__September/October.__ We have taken Margaret + helen out for 3 teas/lunches in the van.

__October 16th Friday.__
1.50. Simon, Eve & Tonic left for a w/end holiday. Bright sunshine on the M5 down past Bristol. We arrived at 5.40. at __Halse Farm campsite__ * * * * (middle of Exmoor) It's nice not having to wait until 4.10. to leave, when the children finish school. This is a working farm - cows, sheep, chicken + 2 barking sheep dogs. The farmer has boarded over the tractor wheels flush with the tyre, to keep mud out.

__Saturday__
A cold frost in the night. I had to get up twice to put more clothes on. We went on a 4 3/4 mile walk along Winsford Hill, down to Winsford village. Friendly P.O. good lunch. Walk back up the hill - one of the nicest I've been on, along a stream & up a very, very old track. No steep climbing. Tea. Now we're sitting in the sun with the van door open.

captions: Looking up the Punch Bowl, Winsford Hill. We walked around this.
Winsford Village - we walked through this, lots of bridges.
The Royal Oak Inn, Winsford where we had lunch - the peanut butter ice cream
pages.text:
Looking up the Punch Bowl, Winsford Hill. We walked around this.

Winsford Village - we walked through this, lots of bridges.

The Royal Oak Inn, Winsford where we had lunch - the peanut butter ice cream

On the walk across the moorland we saw stacks of large hay bales, but when we got up close we saw that they had, in fact, baled the local vegetation - bracken + heather. It would make marvellous walls for sheep shelters? Edible?
	Simon had gone out to look at the stars which were v. bright on the friday night, however on the Saturday night the clouds came.
__Sunday__ The night was very cold. We wore hats + socks. I got too hot in the night + took of my jeans + socks + then regretted it.
	We had switched to red gas - propane - but it ran out (the same can that we had had all along) + the blue gas can barely work. Fortunately the heater stayed on while I got dressed. It was cold at breakfast + I couldn't cook toast.
	On the journey we stopped at to look at an engine house in the Brandon Hills. It hauled a cable for a train up to the mines. This was the top of the cable [illegible]. The old [?Gindling] had metal bars sticking out of the floor for
